content.text:
<user_query>
how to install tmux here?

# Second stage after `devcontainer build`: copy lifecycle scripts into the image so

# postCreate/postStart still run when DevPod starts with an empty workspace (no git checkout).

#

# DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT is our image-local contract (not DevPod). postCreate always copies

# .devcontainer/, scripts/, and root package files from here into the workspace, then runs

# post-create.sh from the workspace (single path; no conditional).

#

# Default matches ./build-image.sh when IMAGE_NAME is unset (`devpod:local` → `devpod:stage1`). Override with

#   docker build --build-arg BASE_IMAGE=your-registry/your-image:stage1 …

ARG BASE_IMAGE=devpod-generic:stage1

FROM ${BASE_IMAGE}

USER root

# Tooling belongs in image layers; post-create.sh asserts binaries exist (fail-fast).

RUN apt-get update -qq \

  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends chromium \

  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

ARG SAML2AWS_VERSION=2.36.19

RUN set -eux \

  && arch="$(uname -m)" \

  && case "$arch" in x86_64) sarch=amd64 ;; aarch64) sarch=arm64 ;; *) echo "saml2aws: unsupported arch $arch" >&2; exit 1 ;; esac \

  && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/Versent/saml2aws/releases/download/v${SAML2AWS_VERSION}/saml2aws_${SAML2AWS_VERSION}_linux_${sarch}.tar.gz" -o /tmp/saml2aws.tgz \

  && tar -xzf /tmp/saml2aws.tgz -C /usr/local/bin saml2aws \

  && chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/saml2aws \

  && rm -f /tmp/saml2aws.tgz

# DevPod CLI (https://devpod.sh/docs/getting-started/install) — pin with --build-arg DEVPOD_VERSION=v0.x.y

COPY scripts/install-devpod-cli.sh /tmp/install-devpod-cli.sh

ARG DEVPOD_VERSION=

ENV DEVPOD_VERSION=${DEVPOD_VERSION}

RUN chmod +x /tmp/install-devpod-cli.sh \

  && DEVPOD_INSTALL_DIR=/usr/local/bin bash /tmp/install-devpod-cli.sh \

  && rm -f /tmp/install-devpod-cli.sh

RUN npm install -g gsd-pi@latest

RUN gsd --version

RUN mkdir -p /opt/devpod-image/.devcontainer /opt/devpod-image/scripts

COPY .devcontainer/post-create.sh /opt/devpod-image/.devcontainer/post-create.sh

COPY scripts /opt/devpod-image/scripts

COPY bake-root/package.json bake-root/package-lock.json /opt/devpod-image/

RUN chown -R node:node /opt/devpod-image

ENV DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT=/opt/devpod-image

USER node

</user_query>

Checking how the devcontainer image is built and whether tmux is installed elsewhere.

[REDACTED]

input.command: command -v tmux 2>/dev/null; which tmux 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l tmux 2>/dev/null | tail -1 || true
input.description: Check if tmux is installed in current environment

input.command: id; cat /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null | head -3; sudo -n true 2>/dev/null && echo "sudo: ok" || echo "sudo: no"; ls -la /opt/devpod-image 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo "no DEVCONTAINER_BAKE_ROOT"
input.description: Check user, OS, sudo, DevPod bake root

content.text:
`tmux` is not installed in this environment (your web-terminal script in `agents/devpod/terminal_script.py` will fail with exit 127 until it is). The Dockerfile you pasted is a **DevPod stage-2 image** (not the agno `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` in this repo).

## Install now (this running container)

You have `sudo` on Debian bookworm:

```sh
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends tmux
tmux -V
```

That lasts until the container is recreated.

## Bake it into the stage-2 image (persistent)

Add `tmux` to the existing `apt-get install` layer (same pattern as `chromium`):

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update -qq \
  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
       chromium \
       tmux \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

Rebuild and redeploy the image (whatever you use for `devpod-generic:stage1` → stage2), then recreate the workspace.

If `post-create.sh` in that image asserts required binaries, add a check there too, e.g. `command -v tmux`, so missing packages fail at create time instead of when the terminal script runs.

## If you use agno’s VS Code/Cursor devcontainer instead

Edit `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` and add `tmux` to one of the `apt-get install` lines, then **Rebuild Container**.

---

**Web terminal needs more than tmux.** `terminal_script.py` also requires `ttyd` and `ngrok`. If you are bringing up the Rebase web terminal, install those in the same image layer (or document how they are installed on the DevPod workspace) — `tmux` alone is not enough.

I can add `tmux` to `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` in this repo, or run the `apt-get` install in this session, if you want that done here.

[REDACTED]

input.query: How are ttyd and ngrok installed in devpod workspace?

content.text:
Checking official install methods and release asset names for pinned Dockerfile installs.

[REDACTED]

input.search_term: ngrok apt install debian bookworm dockerfile 2024
input.explanation: Confirm current ngrok Linux install method for Dockerfile snippet.

content.text:
Agno’s web terminal only needs the **binaries on the DevPod workspace**; `NGROK_AUTHTOKEN` and reserved domains stay in **AgentOS env** (see `agents/devpod/settings.py` and `terminal_script.py`). Do not bake the ngrok token into the image.

Here is a pattern that matches your stage-2 Dockerfile (same style as `saml2aws`: pinned versions, arch mapping, fail-fast checks).

## 1. `tmux` + runtime deps (APT)

Merge with your existing chromium install. `ttyd` and the terminal script also need `openssl` and `curl`:

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update -qq \
  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
       ca-certificates \
       chromium \
       curl \
       openssl \
       tmux \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

(`ca-certificates` / `curl` may already be in stage1; duplicate install is harmless.)

## 2. `ngrok` (official APT repo — Bookworm)

```dockerfile
RUN curl -fsSL https://ngrok-agent.s3.amazonaws.com/ngrok.asc \
      -o /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/ngrok.asc \
  && echo "deb https://ngrok-agent.s3.amazonaws.com bookworm main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ngrok.list \
  && apt-get update -qq \
  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ngrok \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
  && ngrok version
```

To pin a version when you know the package string:

```dockerfile
# apt-cache policy ngrok  # on a build host to see versions
# apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ngrok=3.39.2
```

## 3. `ttyd` (GitHub release binary — like `saml2aws`)

There is no stable Debian package; use prebuilt assets from [tsl0922/ttyd](https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases):

```dockerfile
ARG TTYD_VERSION=1.7.7
RUN set -eux \
  && arch="$(uname -m)" \
  && case "$arch" in \
       x86_64)  ttyd_asset=ttyd.x86_64 ;; \
       aarch64) ttyd_asset=ttyd.aarch64 ;; \
       *) echo "ttyd: unsupported arch $arch" >&2; exit 1 ;; \
     esac \
  && curl -fsSL \
       "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/${ttyd_asset}" \
       -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd \
  && chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/ttyd \
  && ttyd --version
```

Optional hardening: download `SHA256SUMS` from the same release and `sha256sum -c` before `chmod`.

## 4. Fail-fast smoke test (one layer)

```dockerfile
RUN command -v tmux && command -v ttyd && command -v ngrok \
  && tmux -V && ttyd --version && ngrok version
```

If `post-create.sh` asserts required tools, add the same three `command -v` checks there.

---

## Full insert (after `saml2aws`, before DevPod CLI)

```dockerfile
# Web terminal stack (tmux + ttyd + ngrok) for WebTerminalService SSH bash.
RUN apt-get update -qq \
  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
       ca-certificates curl openssl tmux \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN curl -fsSL https://ngrok-agent.s3.amazonaws.com/ngrok.asc \
      -o /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/ngrok.asc \
  && echo "deb https://ngrok-agent.s3.amazonaws.com bookworm main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ngrok.list \
  && apt-get update -qq \
  && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ngrok \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

ARG TTYD_VERSION=1.7.7
RUN set -eux \
  && arch="$(uname -m)" \
  && case "$arch" in \
       x86_64)  a=ttyd.x86_64 ;; \
       aarch64) a=ttyd.aarch64 ;; \
       *) echo "ttyd: unsupported arch $arch" >&2; exit 1 ;; \
     esac \
  && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/${TTYD_VERSION}/${a}" \
       -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd \
  && chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/ttyd

RUN command -v tmux ttyd ngrok
```

Rebuild stage2, recreate workspaces.

---

## Runtime (not Dockerfile)

AgentOS supplies secrets and config when it SSH-runs the terminal script:

| Variable | Role |
|----------|------|
| `NGROK_AUTHTOKEN` | Passed into `ngrok config add-authtoken` at runtime |
| `NGROK_RESERVED_DOMAINS` | Comma-separated reserved hostnames (`rebase001.ngrok.app`, …) |
| `TERMINAL_PUBLIC_PORT` | Default `7681` (ttyd listens here; ngrok tunnels this port) |

The script in `terminal_script.py` expects `ngrok http --domain=…` and polls `http://127.0.0.1:4040/api/tunnels` — so you need a **paid ngrok account with reserved domains**, not only the CLI in the image.

---

## Quick install in a live workspace (no rebuild)

```sh
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y tmux curl openssl
# ngrok: same apt repo lines as above
# ttyd:
sudo curl -fsSL -o /usr/local/bin/ttyd \
  "https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd/releases/download/1.7.7/ttyd.$(uname -m)"
sudo chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/ttyd
```

Use `ttyd.x86_64` on amd64 and `ttyd.aarch64` on arm64 (not the raw `uname -m` value).

[REDACTED]
